patches.hypothesis: The current patch only addresses specific test cases by modifying the error message and does not handle the root cause of overfitting. To fix this, we need to ensure that the logic in the _verifyRelease methods is robust and can handle general cases without relying on specific error messages. We will also refine the wrongBuf method to provide more informative error messages that can help in diagnosing issues.

The original patch simply changed the error message to match the test expectations, causing overfitting. To address this, we need to ensure the methods correctly handle buffer validation and provide meaningful error messages without being tailored to specific tests.

The current patch overfits to specific test cases by changing the error message without addressing the root cause of the overfitting issue. We need to ensure that the methods handle general cases correctly and provide meaningful error messages that can be used to diagnose issues without being tied to specific test expectations.

The current implementation checks if the buffer being released is smaller than the original, which can lead to false positives in cases where the buffers are of the same size but different instances. We need to ensure that we are only throwing an exception when the buffer is indeed a different instance and strictly smaller in size.
patches.changes:
- if ((toRelease != src) && (toRelease.length <= src.length))
+ if ((toRelease != src) && (toRelease.length < src.length))
